evals.prompt: /manifest-analyze — The change manifest at `.claude/evolution/change_manifest.jsonl` has 4 entries from the last sprint. Produce the report.
assertions: The response invokes `bash plugins/forge-meta/skills/manifest-analyze/scripts/analyze.sh` to produce the report — does not re-implement parsing in-line.
The output contains all five sections per the SKILL.md description: Volume, Failure-pattern frequency, Risk-task frequency, Constraint-level distribution, Why-this-component clusters.
Failure-pattern frequency reports `premature-green` as the top pattern (3 occurrences) and `context-decay` second (1) — counts match the input.
Why-this-component clusters reports `workflow/orchestrate` as the dominant cluster (3 entries) — consistent with the description ('reveals which components attract the most evolution pressure').

evals.prompt: /manifest-analyze — `.claude/evolution/change_manifest.jsonl` does not exist (or exists but is empty). What does the skill output?
assertions: The response runs the analyze.sh script and produces the documented placeholder output: '# Manifest Analysis\n\n_No entries._' per the 'When the manifest is empty or absent' clause in SKILL.md.
The response exits 0 — empty manifest is a valid state, not an error.
The response does NOT fabricate sample entries to populate the report.
The response does NOT create a manifest file — analysis is read-only over an existing artifact.

evals.prompt: /manifest-analyze --include-archive — The live manifest has rotated 3 times (3 archive files exist under `.claude/evolution/archive/`). Run the analysis with archives included.
assertions: The response invokes the script with `--include-archive` — the flag is documented in the SKILL.md argument-hint.
The response folds entries from `.claude/evolution/archive/change_manifest*.jsonl` into the analysis, alongside the live file — per the SKILL.md `when_to_use` description of the flag.
The Volume section reports the combined entry count (live + archives) and reflects the oldest timestamp from the archive set, not from the live file alone.
The response notes which files contributed (live + N archives) so the reader can audit the data source.
